prompt.raw:
I am a Search Engineer migrating from Solr to OpenSearch.

Here is my Solr schema:
<schema name="ecommerce" version="1.6">
  <fieldType name="string" class="solr.StrField" sortMissingLast="true"/>
  <fieldType name="pint" class="solr.IntPointField" docValues="true"/>
  <fieldType name="text_general" class="solr.TextField" positionIncrementGap="100">
    <analyzer type="index">
      <tokenizer class="solr.StandardTokenizerFactory"/>
      <filter class="solr.LowerCaseFilterFactory"/>
    </analyzer>
  </fieldType>
  <fieldType name="location" class="solr.LatLonPointSpatialField" docValues="true"/>
  <fieldType name="pdate" class="solr.DatePointField" docValues="true"/>

  <field name="id" type="string" indexed="true" stored="true" required="true"/>
  <field name="title" type="text_general" indexed="true" stored="true"/>
  <field name="price" type="pint" indexed="true" stored="true"/>
  <field name="location" type="location" indexed="true" stored="true"/>
  <field name="created_at" type="pdate" indexed="true" stored="true"/>
  <copyField source="title" dest="title_sort"/>
</schema>

Here are my representative queries:
- title:opensearch AND price:[10 TO 100]

I use SolrJ as my client library.

Please convert the schema, translate the queries, and generate
a complete migration report including the stage plan.
prompt.label: Full migration report request

response.output:
{"output": "# Solr → OpenSearch Migration Report: `ecommerce`

## 1. Schema Review

Your pre-converted mapping has one gap. The `copyField` directive is missing:

```xml
<copyField source="title" dest="title_sort"/>
```

This is a common pattern for sorting on a text field. In OpenSearch, handle this with a `keyword` sub-field (preferred over `copy_to` for sort):

**Corrected mapping:**

```json
{
  "settings": {
    "analysis": {
      "analyzer": {
        "text_general_index": {
          "type": "custom",
          "tokenizer": "standard",
          "filter": ["lowercase"]
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "mappings": {
    "properties": {
      "id":         { "type": "keyword" },
      "title":      {
        "type": "text",
        "analyzer": "text_general_index",
        "fields": {
          "sort": { "type": "keyword" }
        }
      },
      "price":      { "type": "integer" },
      "location":   { "type": "geo_point" },
      "created_at": { "type": "date" }
    }
  }
}
```

**Field-by-field notes:**

| Solr Field | Solr Type | OpenSearch Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | `solr.StrField` | `keyword` | Use as `_id` at index time via `PUT /ecommerce/_doc/{id}` |
| `title` | `text_general` (standard + lowercase) | `text` + custom analyzer | Explicit analyzer preserves exact Solr chain. Default `standard` analyzer also lowercases, but being explicit avoids drift. |
| `price` | `solr.IntPointField` (docValues=true) | `integer` | `doc_values: true` is the OpenSearch default — no action needed |
| `location` | `solr.LatLonPointSpatialField` | `geo_point` | Coordinate order differs: Solr uses `lat,lon`; OpenSearch accepts `{"lat":x,"lon":y}` or GeoJSON `[lon,lat]`. Verify your indexing code. |
| `created_at` | `solr.DatePointField` | `date` | ISO 8601 format, compatible |
| `title_sort` (copyField target) | implied keyword sort | `title.sort` sub-field | Sort with `{"title.sort": "asc"}` instead of a separate field |

---

## 2. Incompatibility Analysis

### Breaking

| # | Category | Description | Recommendation |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **copyField** | `<copyField source="title" dest="title_sort"/>` has no equivalent in the pre-converted mapping. Sorting on `title` as `text` will fail. | Add a `.sort` keyword sub-field on `title` (shown in corrected mapping above). Sort queries use `title.sort` instead of `title_sort`. |
| 2 | **Spatial coordinate order** | Solr `LatLonPointSpatialField` accepts `lat,lon` string. OpenSearch `geo_point` uses `{"lat":x,"lon":y}` object or GeoJSON `[lon,lat]` (longitude first). Indexing with Solr's `lat,lon` string format works, but GeoJSON array order is reversed. | Audit indexing code. If you pass coordinates as `"lat,lon"` strings, OpenSearch handles this correctly. If you use arrays, swap to `[lon, lat]`. |

### Behavioral

| # | Category | Description | Recommendation |
|---|---|---|---|
| 3 | **Relevance scoring** | Solr defaults to TF-IDF (ClassicSimilarity); OpenSearch defaults to BM25. BM25 saturates term frequency, so documents with repeated terms in `title` will score differently. Expect measurable top-10 differences. | Establish a relevance baseline using Quepid with your top queries before cutover. Tune BM25 `k1`/`b` per-field if needed. See §6 below. |
| 4 | **sortMissingLast** | Solr `sortMissingLast="true"` on `StrField`. OpenSearch sorts missing `keyword` values last by default on ascending sort, matching Solr behavior. On descending sort, missing values sort first — same as Solr `sortMissingLast`. | No action needed. Behavior is equivalent for `keyword` fields. |
| 5 | **Commit/refresh semantics** | Solr soft commit ≈ OpenSearch refresh. Default `refresh_interval` is `1s`, so documents are not instantly searchable after indexing (unlike Solr's autoCommit). | Set `refresh_interval` to match your latency requirements. During bulk migration, disable (`"-1"`) and re-enable after. |

### Summary: 2 Breaking, 3 Behavioral, 0 Unsupported

---

## 3. Query Translation

**Source query:** `title:opensearch AND price:[10 TO 100]`

```json
{
  "query": {
    "bool": {
      "must": [
        { "match": { "title": "opensearch" } },
        { "range": { "price": { "gte": 10, "lte": 100 } } }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

**Translation notes:**

- `title:opensearch` → `match` query (analyzed, single term — equivalent to Solr standard parser field query)
- `price:[10 TO 100]` → `range` with `gte`/`lte` (Solr `[` = inclusive = `gte`/`lte`)
- `AND` → `bool.must` (both clauses required)
- The `range` on `price` could be moved to `bool.filter` if it should not affect scoring (common for price filters):

```json
{
  "query": {
    "bool": {
      "must": [
        { "match": { "title": "opensearch" } }
      ],
      "filter": [
        { "range": { "price": { "gte": 10, "lte": 100 } } }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

This is the idiomatic OpenSearch pattern — price range doesn't contribute to relevance, and `filter` context enables caching. Recommend this version.

**Query incompatibility:** Scoring will differ due to BM25 vs TF-IDF (Incompatibility #3 above). For a single-term query like `opensearch`, the practical difference is typically small, but verify with your actual corpus.

---

## 4. Client Migration: SolrJ → opensearch-java

**Dependency change:**

```xml
<!-- Remove -->
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.apache.solr</groupId>
  <artifactId>solr-solrj</artifactId>
</dependency>

<!-- Add -->
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.opensearch.client</groupId>
  <artifactId>opensearch-java</artifactId>
  <version>2.18.0</version>
</dependency>
```

**Client init — before (SolrJ):**
```java
SolrClient solrClient = new HttpSolrClient.Builder(
    "http://localhost:8983/solr/ecommerce"
).build();
```

**Client init — after (opensearch-java):**
```java
RestClientTransport transport = new RestClientTransport(
    RestClient.builder(new HttpHost("localhost", 9200)).build(),
    new JacksonJsonpMapper()
);
OpenSearchClient client = new OpenSearchClient(transport);
```

**Query — before (SolrJ):**
```java
SolrQuery query = new SolrQuery("title:opensearch AND price:[10 TO 100]");
QueryResponse response = solrClient.query(query);
```

**Query — after (opensearch-java):**
```java
SearchResponse<Product> response = client.search(s -> s
    .index("ecommerce")
    .query(q -> q.bool(b -> b
        .must(m -> m.match(mp -> mp.field("title").query("opensearch")))
        .filter(f -> f.range(r -> r.field("price")
            .gte(JsonData.of(10)).lte(JsonData.of(100))))
    )),
    Product.class
);
```

**Key differences:**
- SolrJ uses Solr query strings; opensearch-java uses type-safe DSL builders — full rewrite required
- No ZooKeeper dependency (SolrCloud `CloudSolrClient` → direct HTTP to cluster)
- Response model changes: `SolrDocumentList` → `SearchResponse<T>.hits().hits()`
- Connection pooling: configure `maxConnPerRoute` and `maxConnTotal` on `RestClient.builder()` to match your current SolrJ pool settings

---

## 5. Cluster Sizing Guidance

*(You haven't provided topology details yet. Provide these when ready and I'll give specific recommendations:)*

- Standalone Solr or SolrCloud? Node count, shards, replicas?
- Approximate document count and index size?
- Peak QPS and indexing rate?

**General recommendations for an e-commerce search index:**
- Target shard size: 10–30 GB
- 3 dedicated master nodes (m6g.large or equivalent)
- Multi-AZ deployment (3 AZs)
- JVM heap: 50% of RAM, max 32 GB

---

## 6. Relevance Baseline Plan

Since you're a Search Engineer, this is critical before cutover:

1. **Collect top 100–200 queries** from query logs (by frequency + category coverage)
2. **Build judgment set** in Quepid: 4-point scale (Perfect/Good/Fair/Bad), 2–3 judges, calibrate on 20–30 queries first
3. **Measure nDCG\@10** on current Solr as your baseline
4. **Shadow query** the same set against OpenSearch after indexing
5. **Compare top-10 overlap** — expect differences from BM25. Tune `k1`/`b` per-field if nDCG drops >2%
6. **Decision gate:** Do not cut over until OpenSearch nDCG\@10 ≥ Solr baseline − 2%

---

## 7. Migration Stage Plan

| Stage | Milestone | Blockers | Effort Estimate |
|---|---|---|---|
| **1. Index Setup** | Create OpenSearch index with corrected mapping (§1) | None | 0.5 day |
| **2. Data Migration** | Bulk index all documents; validate doc count match | Coordinate order audit for `location` field (Incompatibility #2) | 1–2 days |
| **3. Query Translation** | Port all queries to OpenSearch DSL; validate results | BM25 scoring differences (Incompatibility #3) | 1–2 days |
| **4. Client Migration** | Replace SolrJ with opensearch-java in application code | Full rewrite of query construction + response parsing | 2–3 days |
| **5. Relevance Validation** | Quepid baseline comparison; tune BM25 if needed | Judgment set must exist; requires query logs | 1–2 weeks |
| **6. Shadow Traffic** | Dual-write or shadow queries to OpenSearch; compare latency and results | Requires infrastructure for parallel routing | 1–2 weeks |
| **7. Cutover** | Switch production traffic to OpenSearch; keep Solr as fallback for 1 week | All prior stages complete; nDCG gate passed | 1 day |

**Total estimated effort:** 4–6 weeks including relevance validation.

---

## Open Items

1. **Provide cluster topology** (node count, doc count, index size, QPS) for specific sizing recommendations
2. **Confirm coordinate format** used when indexing `location` — string `"lat,lon"` vs array vs object
3. **Any Solr customizations?** Custom request handlers, plugins, UpdateRequestProcessorChains, auth setup?
4. **Sort queries** — are you currently sorting by `title_sort`? If so, update to `title.sort` in OpenSearch

Want me to drill into any section?"}

testCase.description: Schema + queries → complete migration report with stage plan
